videos.title: FLUMINENSE 3 X 1 CORINTHIANS | MELHORES MOMENTOS | CAMPEONATO BRASILEIRO 2026
videos.description:
NOITE QUENTE NO MARACANÃ! 

O #Fluminense venceu o #Corinthians por 3 a 1 pela 9ª rodada do #CampeonatoBrasileiro. Em uma noite muito quente no Maracanã, os comandados de #Zubeldia foram muito superiores ao time de #DorivalJr, conquistaram três pontos e seguem na briga pelo topo da tabela da competição. 

Na primeira etapa, o Fluzão abriu o placar com #JohnKennedy e ampliou com #Hercules. Com jogadas bem trabalhadas, o Time de Guerreiros dominou o jogo e fez o goleiro #Fábio trabalhar muito pouco. 

Já nos 45 finais, os donos da casa seguiram com total controle da partida e o argentino #Castillo saiu do banco e fez o terceiro. O Alvinegro do Parque São Jorge diminuiu para os visitantes, mas não foi o suficiente para esboçar uma reação no Rio de Janeiro. 

Campeonato Brasileiro tá no Modo Prime e toda rodada é um jogo exclusivo aqui no #PrimeVideo! No domingo (5), temos Corinthians x Internacional. Esporte ao vivo, o ano todo, TÁ NO AMAZON PRIME!

⚽ Placar final: Fluminense 3 x 1 Corinthians

videos.title: FLUMINENSE 3 X 1 CORINTHIANS | MELHORES MOMENTOS | CAMPEONATO BRASILEIRO 2026
videos.description:
NOITE QUENTE NO MARACANÃ! 

O #Fluminense venceu o #Corinthians por 3 a 1 pela 9ª rodada do #CampeonatoBrasileiro. Em uma noite muito quente no Maracanã, os comandados de #Zubeldia foram muito superiores ao time de #DorivalJr, conquistaram três pontos e seguem na briga pelo topo da tabela da competição. 

Na primeira etapa, o Fluzão abriu o placar com #JohnKennedy e ampliou com #Hercules. Com jogadas bem trabalhadas, o Time de Guerreiros dominou o jogo e fez o goleiro #Fábio trabalhar muito pouco. 

Já nos 45 finais, os donos da casa seguiram com total controle da partida e o argentino #Castillo saiu do banco e fez o terceiro. O Alvinegro do Parque São Jorge diminuiu para os visitantes, mas não foi o suficiente para esboçar uma reação no Rio de Janeiro. 

Campeonato Brasileiro tá no Modo Prime e toda rodada é um jogo exclusivo aqui no #PrimeVideo! No domingo (5), temos Corinthians x Internacional. Esporte ao vivo, o ano todo, TÁ NO AMAZON PRIME!

⚽ Placar final: Fluminense 3 x 1 Corinthians

videos.title: FLUMINENSE 3 X 1 CORINTHIANS | MELHORES MOMENTOS | CAMPEONATO BRASILEIRO 2026
videos.description:
NOITE QUENTE NO MARACANÃ! 

O #Fluminense venceu o #Corinthians por 3 a 1 pela 9ª rodada do #CampeonatoBrasileiro. Em uma noite muito quente no Maracanã, os comandados de #Zubeldia foram muito superiores ao time de #DorivalJr, conquistaram três pontos e seguem na briga pelo topo da tabela da competição. 

Na primeira etapa, o Fluzão abriu o placar com #JohnKennedy e ampliou com #Hercules. Com jogadas bem trabalhadas, o Time de Guerreiros dominou o jogo e fez o goleiro #Fábio trabalhar muito pouco. 

Já nos 45 finais, os donos da casa seguiram com total controle da partida e o argentino #Castillo saiu do banco e fez o terceiro. O Alvinegro do Parque São Jorge diminuiu para os visitantes, mas não foi o suficiente para esboçar uma reação no Rio de Janeiro. 

Campeonato Brasileiro tá no Modo Prime e toda rodada é um jogo exclusivo aqui no #PrimeVideo! No domingo (5), temos Corinthians x Internacional. Esporte ao vivo, o ano todo, TÁ NO AMAZON PRIME!

⚽ Placar final: Fluminense 3 x 1 Corinthians

videos.title: Game Theory #18: Trump World Order
videos.description:
In this Thursday, April 2, 2026 lecture to his Beijing high school students, Professor Jiang explains Donald Trump's grand plan.  

Notes and References:
1.  "Foundations of Geo-Politics" by Aleksandr Dugin

videos.title: Presidential Address to the Nation: ABC News Special
videos.description:
President Donald Trump addresses the nation.

Subscribe to ABC News on YouTube: https://abcnews.visitlink.me/59aJ1G

ABC News Digital is your daily source of breaking national and world news, exclusive interviews and 24/7 live streaming coverage.

Download the ABC News app for the latest headlines and alerts: https://abcnews.go.com/devices

Watch 24/7 coverage of breaking news and live events on ABC News Live:    • LIVE: Latest News Headlines and Events l A...   

Watch full episodes of World News Tonight with David Muir here:    • 2024 | ABC World News Tonight with David M...   

Read ABC News reports online: http://abcnews.go.com

ABC News is the home to the #1 evening newscast “World News Tonight” with David Muir, “Good Morning America,” “20/20,” “Nightline,” “This Week” with George Stephanopoulos, “ABC News Live Prime” with Linsey Davis, plus the daily news podcast “Start Here.”

Connect with ABC News on social media: 

Facebook:   / abcnews  
Instagram:   / abcnews  
TikTok:   / abcnews  
X:   / abc  
Threads: https://www.threads.net/@abcnews
WhatsApp: https://whatsapp.com/channel/0029VajT...
LinkedIn:   / abcnews

IT IS THE FIRST TIME PRESIDENT TRUMP IS SPEAKING TO THE AMERICAN PEOPLE IN PRIMETIME SINCE LAUNCHING THE WAR, ALMOST FIVE WEEKS AGO. JUST THIS WEEK, THE PRESIDENT OUTLINED A POTENTIAL END TO THE WAR, OFFERING A TIMELINE OF 2 TO 3 WEEKS, BUT IT'S NOT CLEAR WHICH CONDITIONS NEED TO BE MET FOR THE PRESIDENT TO END THE CONFLICT. THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ REMAINS CLOSED, THOUGH IRAN IS ALLOWING SOME SHIPS TO GO THROUGH. JUST TODAY, PRESIDENT TRUMP SAID IRAN IS ASKING FOR A CEASE FIRE, BUT THAT THE U.S. WOULD ONLY CONSIDER IT AFTER THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ IS OPEN, FREE AND CLEAR. BUT IRAN RESPONDED THAT THEY HAVEN'T ASKED FOR A CEASE FIRE, AND ONE TOP OFFICIAL SAID THE STRAIT WILL BE OPEN, BUT NOT FOR YOU. THE CLOSURE OF THE STRAIT SENDING FUEL PRICES WORLDWIDE SOARING AND GAS PRICES HERE AT HOME SKYROCKETING TO MORE THAN $4 A GALLON. NEW POLLING SHOWS THAT MANY ARE EAGER FOR JUST THAT. 60% OF AMERICANS DISAPPROVE OF THE IRAN WAR, AND TWO THIRDS SAY THAT THE U.S. SHOULD WORK TO END THE CONFLICT QUICKLY. BUT PRESIDENT TRUMP REPEATEDLY INSISTS THAT THE OPERATION IS SUCCEEDING, AND THE GOAL OF ENSURING THAT IRAN WILL NOT BE ABLE TO BUILD A NUCLEAR WEAPON HAS BEEN ACHIEVED. EARLIER TODAY, THE PRESIDENT EXPRESSED HIS FRUSTRATIONS WITH NATO ALLIES WHO HAVE REFUSED TO JOIN THE WAR EFFORT, SAYING THE U.S. MIGHT EXIT THE NATO ALLIANCE. LET'S TAKE A LISTEN. >> THANK YOU VERY MUCH, MY FELLOW AMERICANS. GOOD EVENING. LET ME BEGIN BY CONGRATULATING THE TEAM AT NASA AND OUR BRAVE ASTRONAUTS ON THE SUCCESSFUL LAUNCH OF ARTEMIS TWO. IT WAS QUITE SOMETHING. IT WOULD BE TRAVELING FURTHER THAN ANY MANNED ROCKET HAS EVER FLOWN, AND WILL VERY SUBSTANTIALLY PASS THE MOON, GO AROUND IT, AND COME BACK HOME FROM A DISTANCE THAT HAS NEVER BEEN DONE BEFORE. IT'S AMAZING THEY ARE ON THE WAY AND GOD BLESS THEM. THESE ARE BRAVE PEOPLE WHO WANT TO. GOD BLESS US FOR UNBELIEVABLE ASTRONAUTS AS WE SPEAK THIS EVENING. IT'S BEEN JUST ONE MONTH SINCE THE UNITED STATES MILITARY BEGAN OPERATION EPIC FURY, TARGETING THE WORLD'S NUMBER ONE STATE SPONSOR OF TERROR, IRAN. IN THESE PAST FOUR WEEKS, OUR ARMED FORCES HAVE DELIVERED SWIFT, DECISIVE, OVERWHELMING VICTORIES ON THE BATTLEFIELD. VICTORIES LIKE FEW PEOPLE HAVE EVER SEEN BEFORE. TONIGHT, IRAN'S NAVY IS GONE. THEIR AIR FORCE IS IN RUINS. THEIR LEADERS, MOST OF THEM TERRORIST REGIME THEY LED, ARE NOW DEAD. THEIR COMMAND AND CONTROL OF THE ISLAMIC REVOLUTIONARY GUARD CORPS IS BEING DECIMATED AS WE SPEAK. videos.title: ABC News Live Prime: Apr. 1, 2026
videos.description:
Pres. Trump addresses public for first time since starting Iran war; Artemis II lifts off for the first crewed journey around the moon since 1972;  Supreme Court justices weigh birthright citizenship.
–––

Subscribe to ABC News on YouTube: https://abcnews.visitlink.me/59aJ1G

ABC News is your daily source of breaking national and world news, exclusive interviews and 24/7 live streaming coverage. https://abcnews.com

Download the ABC News app for the latest headlines and alerts: https://abcnews.com/devices

X:   / abc

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição Well, good evening. I'm Perry Russman for Lindseay Davis. Thank you for streaming with us at home. We begin with President Trump's address tonight. Trump starting off by congratulating the four American astronauts who blasted off toward the moon just hours ago. Trump for the first time since the start of the war against Iran 32 days ago, speaking directly to the American people. Take a listen. Our objectives are very simple and clear. We are systematically dismantling the regime's ability to threaten America or project power outside of their borders. That means eliminating Iran's navy, which is now absolutely destroyed, hurting their air force and their missile program at levels never seen before, and annihilating their defense industrial base. We've done all of it. President Trump did not lay out how the US will determine when it will wrap up the war with Iran. and he went on to say this over the next two to three weeks we're going to bring them back to the stone ages where they belong. In the meantime, discussions are ongoing. Regime change was not our goal. We never said regime change, but regime change has occurred because of all of their original leaders death. They're all dead. The new group is less radical and much more reasonable. Yet, if during this period of time, no deal is made, we have our eyes on key targets. If there is no deal, we are going to hit each and every one of their electric generating plants very hard. Chief Washington correspondent Jonathan Carl joins us now. And John Trump went on to say, "We are getting very close to finishing the job." As for gas prices, he is calling these shortterm increases as gas is going over $4 a gallon nationwide. And he is blaming Iran for that. uh when they attacked other countries in the Middle East. As for the straight of Hormuz, he claims that this does not affect the US. He says other countries will have to grab and cherish it. His words there telling those countries to build up delayed courage and take it. What are your thoughts on what we heard tonight? Uh well, first of all, it really wasn't anything new in the speech. uh this is kind of a collection of of what he has been saying through his truth social posts and some of his public uh statements and expressing you know complete supreme confidence about what will happen uh in Iran. At one point he said again something he has actually said before but that the straight of Hormuz will open up automatically uh once uh the US military operation is over. he uh explained because the Iranians will want to be able to get their oil out on the world market. Uh as for that notion that we shouldn't be concerned about the straight of Harmuz because we don't buy our oil largely, you know, from anything that comes out of the straight. Technically it's correct that we we don't necessarily buy our oil coming through tankers that flow through the straight of Hormuz but it's a global oil market and when uh the price of oil is hundred plus dollars a barrel that is felt around the world including in the United States and it is because of that that we are seeing gas prices uh at at a 2 threeyear high here maybe fouryear high in some places here in the United States. So, uh, look, not much new. The one part of the speech that I thought was the most significant is what you just alluded to at the end where he said, um, that uh, our we we will continue until our objectives are fully achieved. And he said, thanks to the progress we have made, we are on track to complete all of America's military objectives shortly. Very shortly. Now, what does that mean? He goes on to say over the next two to three weeks we are going to be bombing them back into the stone ages. And I thought that one transition was quite remarkable because the very next sentence he says discussions with the Iranians are continuing. So discussions with a nation that you are bombing to the stone ages. Um that kind of sums up Trump. It's the big threats and it's the uh you know offer of of of a very quick resolution. I'm not sure he's going to get to that uh here. Um yeah, and Trump started tonight by saying that he was going to be giving an update on the war, why it's necessary to keep the US involved and keep the world safe. I mean, his message to Americans tonight, one thing I wrote down, he said, "This is an investment in your children's and grandchildren's future. Do you think the message to Americans tonight was clear on why the US is involved?" I mean, the message was clear. Whether or not it'll be convincing to Americans is another question that that the message that he that they have really settled on is that Iran was on the verge of getting a nuclear bomb and that Trump says that they were going to use and use very quickly uh and that uh all that Iran has done and Iran has been a you know a prime state sponsor of terrorism uh in in the Middle East. Iran has done great damage to US interests uh in in in the region. Uh that that they would have nuclear blackmail, the ability to blackmail the United States and the rest of the world if they had a nuclear weapon. Now whether or not Americans find that convincing right now, we've seen, you know, pretty convincing uh poll numbers, not just one poll or two poll or three polls, but all of them over o over over the last few weeks showing that a very solid majority of Americans have deep uneasiness. uh about this war and oppose this war. Uh but he he outlined he thinks that he's done something uh that would protect Americans interests not just in the short term but in the long term by ensuring that Iran won't have a nuclear weapon. That's what he's saying. John Carl, thank you. Let's bring in Martha Ratis from more our chief global affairs anchor. And Martha, President Trump mentioned the 13 American service members who were killed, saying he wants to honor them by finishing the job here. What stood out to you tonight? Well, that certainly stood out, but it is also how disconnected Americans are from this war. And that is one reason that President Trump tried to give this speech and make things clear, which really didn't happen tonight. It is really unprecedented that we would start a war of this scale without a president talking about it or debating it in the public or having Congress involved in some way uh before those uh attacks were launched. We have right now had 300 service members injured, several of them serious in addition to those who have been killed. And the president hasn't really talked about that. The Pentagon does have briefings, but they don't answer a lot of questions and they don't give a lot of information about what is exactly happening to those service members. We know some of our bases have been attacked. We don't really know what the damage is to those bases. Uh we certainly know some of our allies in the region have been attacked. So Iran is not exactly surrendering and giving up here. They are still launching missiles. They are still fighting the United States. And one of the things we keep hearing is this changing timeline from the very beginning. If you remember a few weeks in uh when he was mad at the British prime minister because he didn't want to send people to help open the straight uh he said we don't need your help. we've already won. So, the Americans are getting these all kinds of mixed messages. It's going to be over in a couple of weeks, uh, more than two or two and a half weeks ago. The energy secretary told me it would be over in two weeks, if not less. That has not happened. So, the Americans deserve to know why that hasn't happened, why this timeline has changed, and what really is the objective. You also got to think back that President Trump was angry about the protesters, the tens of thousands of protesters that have been killed by Iran and he made threats about that. But then that changed the objective was then nuclear. The president had said the nuclear weapons program was obliterated last summer. It clearly was not if they have to go back and do it again. And he said that is his objective. And then he says, you know, I'm not worried about the uranium that's buried deep in the earth right now. So I think it is further confusing the American people exactly why this will end, when this will end, and whether the objectives have been met. Perry, and then the latest timeline he gave tonight, Martha, was two to three weeks going forward. And one of the questions we had going into tonight, what would President Trump mention, what would he not mention? One thing he did not mention, troops on the ground. Your thoughts on that? He he did not. And of course we know we have Marines heading there. Some are already in the area and we have the 82nd Airborne in there as well or on their way in some sort of base in the region. Don't know what they're going to do, but we do know according to sources that the president has been briefed on the possibility they could go in and try to get that uranium. That would be an incredibly complicated mission and a very dangerous mission as well. They could go to Car Island. Uh but he did not talk about that. He did say again and again that they would uh take further action if the Iranians do not behave but again no timeline. You say he said two or three weeks. He said that before and that has passed. Martha Radit, thank you. Let's bring in Matt Rivers. He's in the Middle East tonight. So Matt, President Trump said that Iran's navy is gone. The air force is in ruins with nearly 13,000 targets hit so far by the US. So how are strikes in the region looking at the moment? Well, we've seen some of the most intense fighting of this entire war in just the last few days. And that's kind of struck me, Perry, as we continue to go back and forth on the status of negotiations. Who's saying what the president says? Negotiations are going forward. And yet, we continue to see incredibly intense strikes across the region in Iran, in Israel, in Kuwait, in Bahrain, in Qatar. You know, it goes on and on. And what you continue to hear from the Iranians is defiance. The president can say whatever he wants. He can say uh that they are begging for a deal. he can say uh that he believes a deal will happen soon, but at least publicly what we're hearing from the Iranians is nothing but defiance. It was earlier today uh after the president came out and said the the quote new president of Iran uh that he was negotiating with wants a ceasefire. Just a few minutes later, we got a statement from the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps, the heart of that country's military, saying the straight of Hormuz will remain closed, that they will not be uh uh negot or agreeing to any ceasefire anytime soon. And so look, there's no question that Iran's military capabilities have been severely degraded as a result of this. But Iran is not yet defeated. And they know that by keeping the straight of Hormuz closed, it is a point of leverage, the biggest point of leverage that they have. And so they're not going to walk away from that unless they feel like they've kind of have no other choice in the matter or benefits them uh in some way. And then to counter that, I mean, President Trump said tonight, "We have the cards, they have none." He's threatening to bomb them back into the stone age. He says if there is no deal, they will hit their energy facilities, something that they have not done yet, I believe. Matt, correct me on that if I'm wrong, and then even entertain the idea of going after their oil facilities. Your thoughts on that? Well, I mean, you could certainly escalate if you're the United States from from this point. I mean, you heard Martha talking about it. They could use those ground forces in a number of different ways uh in Iran. You could go after energy infrastructure, although we have seen the Israelis go after different infrastructure sites uh within Iran. There's no question that the US could inflict more pain on Iran should it want to do that. But the question is what is the calculation that the United States is going to make and that is something that Iran is very very aware of. They know the political pressures that President Trump is facing. They they see the same poll numbers and approval ratings that we do. And so when you're looking at how the Iranians are going to respond here, they're going to take it right up to the edge. Remember, this is an existential fight for this regime. They don't have a lot of incentive, at least right now, to just give up because for them that means it's over. And so they're going to go down swinging, you have to imagine. Uh could it get a lot worse? Could we see US troops on the ground? Absolutely. But the Iranians could also do even more on their side to make this more painful for the United States and the global economy. All right, Matt Rivers, thank you. Listen, let's bring in ABC News contributor and retired Colonel Steve Ganard. Steve, thank you for joining us here tonight. First off, your reaction to what we heard or maybe didn't hear from President Trump. Yeah, I think one of the things that Perry we did not hear was the uh the nuclear the high highlyenriched uranium and the nuclear facilities and during the day and for the past couple days the administration sort of been playing that down and the president said today I don't really care about it anymore. If we don't we'll go back and do quote spot hits. So I think what may have happened here is in the past few days the US and Israeli attacks have gone after the sites that were previously hit. Natants, uh, Isvahan, uh, Iraq, all those places where the nuclear program was before, it seems that the US and Israel have gone back and hit them again, tried to bury that nuclear capability as deeply as they could. And so what the president was saying was, I've sort of checked the nuclear problem off the off the list because we know that we've buried it, and if we see that they go and try to unbury it with our satellites, then we can go back and tap it again uh with our spot hits, as he said. So, I think it's kind of interesting that they talked about we've we've taken care of the army, the navy, we've taken care of the missile production, uh, and we've taken care of at least notionally their ability to hit Israel and the United States, but the nuclear problem is got that quiet check mark and it was not addressed tonight. Steve, we talked about it with Martha. No mention of troops on the ground. What do you think the likelihood is that we see American troops on the ground in Iran? Uh, given what the president said tonight, it's hard to see why. Why would you want to take the risk? because every time we think about putting uh boots on the ground, we know that that's an exponential kind of risk increase in risk to US forces. So if the water if the war is going as swimmingly as the president said it was and that we have only two or three more weeks, we're sort of winding down. We're sort of in the secondary tertiary target list here. Why put boots on the ground? And the question is what would they do? The one thing I do think is interesting, Perry, is that we're seeing some reports out of southern uh Iran uh around the Bond Abbas area, which is right at the top of the straight of Hormuz. And it seems like that the US and Israel are beginning to focus more attacks down by the mouth of the Gulf. So, is this in preparation to try to take out any resistance, uh any kind of anti-hship missiles, any kind of capability to keep the straight bottled up? We'll see. But clearly if the president is willing to walk away from the straight as he indicated tonight and leave that to the rest of the world that relies on the on the oil that comes out of the straight uh that will be a different uh a different ending to this war than was previously thought of even a couple days ago. Steve Gard, thank you for your time tonight. Let's bring in senior White House correspondent Selena Wang. So Selena, what are your biggest takeaways from Trump's speech tonight? Well, Perry, I think it's worth reiterating that we are five weeks into this war and parts of his prime time address is really what you would have expected the president to do before this conflict began to sell to the American people about why taking this risk was necessary. But now, 5 weeks in, the president is continuing to try and give a justification to the American people about why we took this step. But we didn't really get clear answers about when this is going to wrap up, why exactly when we went into there and what the objectives are going to be in order for the US to pull out. The president giving really no clear path to ending this conflict. We did hear him repeat what he has been repeating over the last several weeks that this was an outstanding military success when it comes to annihilating its navy, getting rid of the ballistic missile threat. Also, uh the nuclear threat, claiming that that has been solved, though, Perry not providing any public evidence to do so as international inspectors are still warning that there is still a lot of highlyenriched uranium buried deep underground. And I think Harry, also worth mentioning that the president barely acknowledged the economic fallout here as so many Americans are feeling the pain at the pump. You know, gas prices, diesel prices skyrocketing. The president claiming this is just a short-term blip. Uh the president really not empathizing when it comes to the economic challenges here. So, let's talk about negotiations, Selena. President Trump saying we have all the cards, they have none. He says we are getting very close to finishing the job. Where do negotiations stand right now? Yeah, Perry. and in the same breath that he says this war is almost over but we are going to continue to hit them hard over the next few weeks. He then says meanwhile talks are ongoing. We really didn't hear a whole lot from the president about these ongoing negotiations. Selena Wang live in Washington. Selena, thank you. Now to our other top story tonight, the successful liftoff for NASA's Aremis 2 rocket. The first crew journey around the moon in more than 50 years. videos.title: ABC News Live Prime: Apr. 1, 2026
videos.description:
Pres. Trump addresses public for first time since starting Iran war; Artemis II lifts off for the first crewed journey around the moon since 1972;  Supreme Court justices weigh birthright citizenship.
–––

Subscribe to ABC News on YouTube: https://abcnews.visitlink.me/59aJ1G

ABC News is your daily source of breaking national and world news, exclusive interviews and 24/7 live streaming coverage. https://abcnews.com

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição Well, good evening. I'm Perry Russman for Lindseay Davis. Thank you for streaming with us at home. We begin with President Trump's address tonight. Trump starting off by congratulating the four American astronauts who blasted off toward the moon just hours ago. Trump for the first time since the start of the war against Iran 32 days ago, speaking directly to the American people. Take a listen. Our objectives are very simple and clear. We are systematically dismantling the regime's ability to threaten America or project power outside of their borders. That means eliminating Iran's navy, which is now absolutely destroyed, hurting their air force and their missile program at levels never seen before, and annihilating their defense industrial base. We've done all of it. President Trump did not lay out how the US will determine when it will wrap up the war with Iran. and he went on to say this over the next two to three weeks we're going to bring them back to the stone ages where they belong. In the meantime, discussions are ongoing. Regime change was not our goal. We never said regime change, but regime change has occurred because of all of their original leaders death. They're all dead. The new group is less radical and much more reasonable. Yet, if during this period of time, no deal is made, we have our eyes on key targets. If there is no deal, we are going to hit each and every one of their electric generating plants very hard. Chief Washington correspondent Jonathan Carl joins us now. And John Trump went on to say, "We are getting very close to finishing the job." As for gas prices, he is calling these shortterm increases as gas is going over $4 a gallon nationwide. And he is blaming Iran for that. uh when they attacked other countries in the Middle East. As for the straight of Hormuz, he claims that this does not affect the US. He says other countries will have to grab and cherish it. His words there telling those countries to build up delayed courage and take it. What are your thoughts on what we heard tonight? Uh well, first of all, it really wasn't anything new in the speech. uh this is kind of a collection of of what he has been saying through his truth social posts and some of his public uh statements and expressing you know complete supreme confidence about what will happen uh in Iran. At one point he said again something he has actually said before but that the straight of Hormuz will open up automatically uh once uh the US military operation is over. he uh explained because the Iranians will want to be able to get their oil out on the world market. Uh as for that notion that we shouldn't be concerned about the straight of Harmuz because we don't buy our oil largely, you know, from anything that comes out of the straight. Technically it's correct that we we don't necessarily buy our oil coming through tankers that flow through the straight of Hormuz but it's a global oil market and when uh the price of oil is hundred plus dollars a barrel that is felt around the world including in the United States and it is because of that that we are seeing gas prices uh at at a 2 threeyear high here maybe fouryear high in some places here in the United States. So, uh, look, not much new. The one part of the speech that I thought was the most significant is what you just alluded to at the end where he said, um, that uh, our we we will continue until our objectives are fully achieved. And he said, thanks to the progress we have made, we are on track to complete all of America's military objectives shortly. Very shortly. Now, what does that mean? He goes on to say over the next two to three weeks we are going to be bombing them back into the stone ages. And I thought that one transition was quite remarkable because the very next sentence he says discussions with the Iranians are continuing. So discussions with a nation that you are bombing to the stone ages. Um that kind of sums up Trump. It's the big threats and it's the uh you know offer of of of a very quick resolution. I'm not sure he's going to get to that uh here. Um yeah, and Trump started tonight by saying that he was going to be giving an update on the war, why it's necessary to keep the US involved and keep the world safe. I mean, his message to Americans tonight, one thing I wrote down, he said, "This is an investment in your children's and grandchildren's future. Do you think the message to Americans tonight was clear on why the US is involved?" I mean, the message was clear. Whether or not it'll be convincing to Americans is another question that that the message that he that they have really settled on is that Iran was on the verge of getting a nuclear bomb and that Trump says that they were going to use and use very quickly uh and that uh all that Iran has done and Iran has been a you know a prime state sponsor of terrorism uh in in the Middle East. Iran has done great damage to US interests uh in in in the region. Uh that that they would have nuclear blackmail, the ability to blackmail the United States and the rest of the world if they had a nuclear weapon. Now whether or not Americans find that convincing right now, we've seen, you know, pretty convincing uh poll numbers, not just one poll or two poll or three polls, but all of them over o over over the last few weeks showing that a very solid majority of Americans have deep uneasiness. uh about this war and oppose this war. Uh but he he outlined he thinks that he's done something uh that would protect Americans interests not just in the short term but in the long term by ensuring that Iran won't have a nuclear weapon. That's what he's saying. John Carl, thank you. Let's bring in Martha Ratis from more our chief global affairs anchor. And Martha, President Trump mentioned the 13 American service members who were killed, saying he wants to honor them by finishing the job here. What stood out to you tonight? Well, that certainly stood out, but it is also how disconnected Americans are from this war. And that is one reason that President Trump tried to give this speech and make things clear, which really didn't happen tonight. It is really unprecedented that we would start a war of this scale without a president talking about it or debating it in the public or having Congress involved in some way uh before those uh attacks were launched. We have right now had 300 service members injured, several of them serious in addition to those who have been killed. And the president hasn't really talked about that. The Pentagon does have briefings, but they don't answer a lot of questions and they don't give a lot of information about what is exactly happening to those service members. We know some of our bases have been attacked. We don't really know what the damage is to those bases. Uh we certainly know some of our allies in the region have been attacked. So Iran is not exactly surrendering and giving up here. They are still launching missiles. They are still fighting the United States. And one of the things we keep hearing is this changing timeline from the very beginning. If you remember a few weeks in uh when he was mad at the British prime minister because he didn't want to send people to help open the straight uh he said we don't need your help. we've already won. So, the Americans are getting these all kinds of mixed messages. It's going to be over in a couple of weeks, uh, more than two or two and a half weeks ago. The energy secretary told me it would be over in two weeks, if not less. That has not happened. So, the Americans deserve to know why that hasn't happened, why this timeline has changed, and what really is the objective. You also got to think back that President Trump was angry about the protesters, the tens of thousands of protesters that have been killed by Iran and he made threats about that. But then that changed the objective was then nuclear. The president had said the nuclear weapons program was obliterated last summer. It clearly was not if they have to go back and do it again. And he said that is his objective. And then he says, you know, I'm not worried about the uranium that's buried deep in the earth right now. So I think it is further confusing the American people exactly why this will end, when this will end, and whether the objectives have been met. Perry, and then the latest timeline he gave tonight, Martha, was two to three weeks going forward. And one of the questions we had going into tonight, what would President Trump mention, what would he not mention? One thing he did not mention, troops on the ground. Your thoughts on that? He he did not. And of course we know we have Marines heading there. Some are already in the area and we have the 82nd Airborne in there as well or on their way in some sort of base in the region. Don't know what they're going to do, but we do know according to sources that the president has been briefed on the possibility they could go in and try to get that uranium. That would be an incredibly complicated mission and a very dangerous mission as well. They could go to Car Island. Uh but he did not talk about that. He did say again and again that they would uh take further action if the Iranians do not behave but again no timeline. You say he said two or three weeks. He said that before and that has passed. Martha Radit, thank you. Let's bring in Matt Rivers. He's in the Middle East tonight. So Matt, President Trump said that Iran's navy is gone. Well, we've seen some of the most intense fighting of this entire war in just the last few days. And that's kind of struck me, Perry, as we continue to go back and forth on the status of negotiations. Who's saying what the president says? Negotiations are going forward. And yet, we continue to see incredibly intense strikes across the region in Iran, in Israel, in Kuwait, in Bahrain, in Qatar. You know, it goes on and on. And what you continue to hear from the Iranians is defiance. The president can say whatever he wants. He can say uh that they are begging for a deal. he can say uh that he believes a deal will happen soon, but at least publicly what we're hearing from the Iranians is nothing but defiance. It was earlier today uh after the president came out and said the the quote new president of Iran uh that he was negotiating with wants a ceasefire. Just a few minutes later, we got a statement from the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps, the heart of that country's military, saying the straight of Hormuz will remain closed, that they will not be uh uh negot or agreeing to any ceasefire anytime soon. And so look, there's no question that Iran's military capabilities have been severely degraded as a result of this. But Iran is not yet defeated. And they know that by keeping the straight of Hormuz closed, it is a point of leverage, the biggest point of leverage that they have. And so they're not going to walk away from that unless they feel like they've kind of have no other choice in the matter or benefits them uh in some way. And then to counter that, I mean, President Trump said tonight, "We have the cards, they have none." He's threatening to bomb them back into the stone age. He says if there is no deal, they will hit their energy facilities, something that they have not done yet, I believe. Matt, correct me on that if I'm wrong, and then even entertain the idea of going after their oil facilities. Your thoughts on that? Well, I mean, you could certainly escalate if you're the United States from from this point. I mean, you heard Martha talking about it. They could use those ground forces in a number of different ways uh in Iran. You could go after energy infrastructure, although we have seen the Israelis go after different infrastructure sites uh within Iran. There's no question that the US could inflict more pain on Iran should it want to do that. But the question is what is the calculation that the United States is going to make and that is something that Iran is very very aware of. They know the political pressures that President Trump is facing. They they see the same poll numbers and approval ratings that we do. And so when you're looking at how the Iranians are going to respond here, they're going to take it right up to the edge. Remember, this is an existential fight for this regime. They don't have a lot of incentive, at least right now, to just give up because for them that means it's over. And so they're going to go down swinging, you have to imagine. Uh could it get a lot worse? Could we see US troops on the ground? Absolutely. But the Iranians could also do even more on their side to make this more painful for the United States and the global economy. All right, Matt Rivers, thank you. Listen, let's bring in ABC News contributor and retired Colonel Steve Ganard. Steve, thank you for joining us here tonight. First off, your reaction to what we heard or maybe didn't hear from President Trump. Yeah, I think one of the things that Perry we did not hear was the uh the nuclear the high highlyenriched uranium and the nuclear facilities and during the day and for the past couple days the administration sort of been playing that down and the president said today I don't really care about it anymore. If we don't we'll go back and do quote spot hits. So I think what may have happened here is in the past few days the US and Israeli attacks have gone after the sites that were previously hit. Natants, uh, Isvahan, uh, Iraq, all those places where the nuclear program was before, it seems that the US and Israel have gone back and hit them again, tried to bury that nuclear capability as deeply as they could. And so what the president was saying was, I've sort of checked the nuclear problem off the off the list because we know that we've buried it, and if we see that they go and try to unbury it with our satellites, then we can go back and tap it again uh with our spot hits, as he said. So, I think it's kind of interesting that they talked about we've we've taken care of the army, the navy, we've taken care of the missile production, uh, and we've taken care of at least notionally their ability to hit Israel and the United States, but the nuclear problem is got that quiet check mark and it was not addressed tonight. Steve, we talked about it with Martha. No mention of troops on the ground. What do you think the likelihood is that we see American troops on the ground in Iran? Uh, given what the president said tonight, it's hard to see why. Além disso, após um julgamento que capturou a atenção do mundo todo, Gisèle Pelicot falou sobre os abusos que sofreu nas mãos do marido e de dezenas de desconhecidos.

While gas prices at home top an average of $4 a gallon, the backlash this morning after President Trump signed an executive order attempting to crack down on mailin voting. Critics calling it unconstitutional with midterms fast approaching. videos.title: ABC News Live Prime: Apr. 1, 2026
videos.description:
Pres. Trump addresses public for first time since starting Iran war; Artemis II lifts off for the first crewed journey around the moon since 1972;  Supreme Court justices weigh birthright citizenship.

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição Well, good evening. I'm Perry Russman for Lindseay Davis. Thank you for streaming with us at home. We begin with President Trump's address tonight. Trump starting off by congratulating the four American astronauts who blasted off toward the moon just hours ago. Trump for the first time since the start of the war against Iran 32 days ago, speaking directly to the American people. Take a listen. Our objectives are very simple and clear. We are systematically dismantling the regime's ability to threaten America or project power outside of their borders. That means eliminating Iran's navy, which is now absolutely destroyed, hurting their air force and their missile program at levels never seen before, and annihilating their defense industrial base. We've done all of it. President Trump did not lay out how the US will determine when it will wrap up the war with Iran. and he went on to say this over the next two to three weeks we're going to bring them back to the stone ages where they belong. In the meantime, discussions are ongoing. Regime change was not our goal. We never said regime change, but regime change has occurred because of all of their original leaders death. They're all dead. The new group is less radical and much more reasonable. Yet, if during this period of time, no deal is made, we have our eyes on key targets. If there is no deal, we are going to hit each and every one of their electric generating plants very hard. Chief Washington correspondent Jonathan Carl joins us now. And John Trump went on to say, "We are getting very close to finishing the job." As for gas prices, he is calling these shortterm increases as gas is going over $4 a gallon nationwide. And he is blaming Iran for that. uh when they attacked other countries in the Middle East. As for the straight of Hormuz, he claims that this does not affect the US. He says other countries will have to grab and cherish it. His words there telling those countries to build up delayed courage and take it. What are your thoughts on what we heard tonight? Uh well, first of all, it really wasn't anything new in the speech. uh this is kind of a collection of of what he has been saying through his truth social posts and some of his public uh statements and expressing you know complete supreme confidence about what will happen uh in Iran. At one point he said again something he has actually said before but that the straight of Hormuz will open up automatically uh once uh the US military operation is over. he uh explained because the Iranians will want to be able to get their oil out on the world market. Uh as for that notion that we shouldn't be concerned about the straight of Harmuz because we don't buy our oil largely, you know, from anything that comes out of the straight. Technically it's correct that we we don't necessarily buy our oil coming through tankers that flow through the straight of Hormuz but it's a global oil market and when uh the price of oil is hundred plus dollars a barrel that is felt around the world including in the United States and it is because of that that we are seeing gas prices uh at at a 2 threeyear high here maybe fouryear high in some places here in the United States. So, uh, look, not much new. The one part of the speech that I thought was the most significant is what you just alluded to at the end where he said, um, that uh, our we we will continue until our objectives are fully achieved. And he said, thanks to the progress we have made, we are on track to complete all of America's military objectives shortly. Very shortly. Now, what does that mean? He goes on to say over the next two to three weeks we are going to be bombing them back into the stone ages. And I thought that one transition was quite remarkable because the very next sentence he says discussions with the Iranians are continuing. So discussions with a nation that you are bombing to the stone ages. Um that kind of sums up Trump. It's the big threats and it's the uh you know offer of of of a very quick resolution. I'm not sure he's going to get to that uh here. Um yeah, and Trump started tonight by saying that he was going to be giving an update on the war, why it's necessary to keep the US involved and keep the world safe. I mean, his message to Americans tonight, one thing I wrote down, he said, "This is an investment in your children's and grandchildren's future. Do you think the message to Americans tonight was clear on why the US is involved?" I mean, the message was clear. Whether or not it'll be convincing to Americans is another question that that the message that he that they have really settled on is that Iran was on the verge of getting a nuclear bomb and that Trump says that they were going to use and use very quickly uh and that uh all that Iran has done and Iran has been a you know a prime state sponsor of terrorism uh in in the Middle East. Iran has done great damage to US interests uh in in in the region. Uh that that they would have nuclear blackmail, the ability to blackmail the United States and the rest of the world if they had a nuclear weapon. Now whether or not Americans find that convincing right now, we've seen, you know, pretty convincing uh poll numbers, not just one poll or two poll or three polls, but all of them over o over over the last few weeks showing that a very solid majority of Americans have deep uneasiness. uh about this war and oppose this war. Uh but he he outlined he thinks that he's done something uh that would protect Americans interests not just in the short term but in the long term by ensuring that Iran won't have a nuclear weapon. That's what he's saying. John Carl, thank you. Let's bring in Martha Ratis from more our chief global affairs anchor. And Martha, President Trump mentioned the 13 American service members who were killed, saying he wants to honor them by finishing the job here. What stood out to you tonight? Well, that certainly stood out, but it is also how disconnected Americans are from this war. And that is one reason that President Trump tried to give this speech and make things clear, which really didn't happen tonight. It is really unprecedented that we would start a war of this scale without a president talking about it or debating it in the public or having Congress involved in some way uh before those uh attacks were launched. We have right now had 300 service members injured, several of them serious in addition to those who have been killed. And the president hasn't really talked about that. The Pentagon does have briefings, but they don't answer a lot of questions and they don't give a lot of information about what is exactly happening to those service members. We know some of our bases have been attacked. We don't really know what the damage is to those bases. Uh we certainly know some of our allies in the region have been attacked. So Iran is not exactly surrendering and giving up here. They are still launching missiles. They are still fighting the United States. And one of the things we keep hearing is this changing timeline from the very beginning. If you remember a few weeks in uh when he was mad at the British prime minister because he didn't want to send people to help open the straight uh he said we don't need your help. we've already won. So, the Americans are getting these all kinds of mixed messages. It's going to be over in a couple of weeks, uh, more than two or two and a half weeks ago. The energy secretary told me it would be over in two weeks, if not less. That has not happened. So, the Americans deserve to know why that hasn't happened, why this timeline has changed, and what really is the objective. You also got to think back that President Trump was angry about the protesters, the tens of thousands of protesters that have been killed by Iran and he made threats about that. But then that changed the objective was then nuclear. The president had said the nuclear weapons program was obliterated last summer. It clearly was not if they have to go back and do it again. And he said that is his objective. And then he says, you know, I'm not worried about the uranium that's buried deep in the earth right now. So I think it is further confusing the American people exactly why this will end, when this will end, and whether the objectives have been met. Perry, and then the latest timeline he gave tonight, Martha, was two to three weeks going forward. And one of the questions we had going into tonight, what would President Trump mention, what would he not mention? One thing he did not mention, troops on the ground. Your thoughts on that? He he did not. And of course we know we have Marines heading there. Some are already in the area and we have the 82nd Airborne in there as well or on their way in some sort of base in the region. Don't know what they're going to do, but we do know according to sources that the president has been briefed on the possibility they could go in and try to get that uranium. That would be an incredibly complicated mission and a very dangerous mission as well. They could go to Car Island. Uh but he did not talk about that. He did say again and again that they would uh take further action if the Iranians do not behave but again no timeline. You say he said two or three weeks. He said that before and that has passed. And that's kind of struck me, Perry, as we continue to go back and forth on the status of negotiations. Who's saying what the president says? Negotiations are going forward. And yet, we continue to see incredibly intense strikes across the region in Iran, in Israel, in Kuwait, in Bahrain, in Qatar. You know, it goes on and on. And what you continue to hear from the Iranians is defiance. The president can say whatever he wants. He can say uh that they are begging for a deal. he can say uh that he believes a deal will happen soon, but at least publicly what we're hearing from the Iranians is nothing but defiance. It was earlier today uh after the president came out and said the the quote new president of Iran uh that he was negotiating with wants a ceasefire. Just a few minutes later, we got a statement from the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps, the heart of that country's military, saying the straight of Hormuz will remain closed, that they will not be uh uh negot or agreeing to any ceasefire anytime soon. And so look, there's no question that Iran's military capabilities have been severely degraded as a result of this. But Iran is not yet defeated. And they know that by keeping the straight of Hormuz closed, it is a point of leverage, the biggest point of leverage that they have. And so they're not going to walk away from that unless they feel like they've kind of have no other choice in the matter or benefits them uh in some way. And then to counter that, I mean, President Trump said tonight, "We have the cards, they have none." He's threatening to bomb them back into the stone age. He says if there is no deal, they will hit their energy facilities, something that they have not done yet, I believe. Matt, correct me on that if I'm wrong, and then even entertain the idea of going after their oil facilities. Your thoughts on that? Well, I mean, you could certainly escalate if you're the United States from from this point. I mean, you heard Martha talking about it. They could use those ground forces in a number of different ways uh in Iran. You could go after energy infrastructure, although we have seen the Israelis go after different infrastructure sites uh within Iran. There's no question that the US could inflict more pain on Iran should it want to do that. But the question is what is the calculation that the United States is going to make and that is something that Iran is very very aware of. They know the political pressures that President Trump is facing. They they see the same poll numbers and approval ratings that we do. And so when you're looking at how the Iranians are going to respond here, they're going to take it right up to the edge. Remember, this is an existential fight for this regime. They don't have a lot of incentive, at least right now, to just give up because for them that means it's over. And so they're going to go down swinging, you have to imagine. Uh could it get a lot worse? Could we see US troops on the ground? Absolutely. But the Iranians could also do even more on their side to make this more painful for the United States and the global economy. All right, Matt Rivers, thank you. Listen, let's bring in ABC News contributor and retired Colonel Steve Ganard. Steve, thank you for joining us here tonight. First off, your reaction to what we heard or maybe didn't hear from President Trump. Yeah, I think one of the things that Perry we did not hear was the uh the nuclear the high highlyenriched uranium and the nuclear facilities and during the day and for the past couple days the administration sort of been playing that down and the president said today I don't really care about it anymore. If we don't we'll go back and do quote spot hits. So I think what may have happened here is in the past few days the US and Israeli attacks have gone after the sites that were previously hit. Natants, uh, Isvahan, uh, Iraq, all those places where the nuclear program was before, it seems that the US and Israel have gone back and hit them again, tried to bury that nuclear capability as deeply as they could. And so what the president was saying was, I've sort of checked the nuclear problem off the off the list because we know that we've buried it, and if we see that they go and try to unbury it with our satellites, then we can go back and tap it again uh with our spot hits, as he said. So, I think it's kind of interesting that they talked about we've we've taken care of the army, the navy, we've taken care of the missile production, uh, and we've taken care of at least notionally their ability to hit Israel and the United States, but the nuclear problem is got that quiet check mark and it was not addressed tonight. Steve, we talked about it with Martha. No mention of troops on the ground. What do you think the likelihood is that we see American troops on the ground in Iran? Uh, given what the president said tonight, it's hard to see why. Why would you want to take the risk? because every time we think about putting uh boots on the ground, we know that that's an exponential kind of risk increase in risk to US forces. We're sort of in the secondary tertiary target list here. Why put boots on the ground? And the question is what would they do? The one thing I do think is interesting, Perry, is that we're seeing some reports out of southern uh Iran uh around the Bond Abbas area, which is right at the top of the straight of Hormuz. Na edição de quarta-feira do News Hour, o presidente Trump sinalizou novamente que a guerra com o Irã poderia terminar em breve, mas deu sinais contraditórios sobre como isso poderia ser alcançado. A Suprema Corte ouviu o caso do governo Trump para acabar com a cidadania por nascimento. Além disso, após um julgamento que capturou a atenção do mundo todo, Gisèle Pelicot falou sobre os abusos que sofreu nas mãos do marido e de dezenas de desconhecidos.
